title: coleus amoenus
intro: Coleus amoenus, also known as Coleus aromaticus, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family and is commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Coleus amoenus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Coleus amoenus are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
